title: Okoume Wood (Aucoumea klaineana): Properties, Density & Uses
summary: Okoume (Aucoumea klaineana) is a light, low-density West African hardwood — the world's leading plywood veneer species. Density, Janka hardness, workability, durability, sustainability and its use in packing and marine plywood, cross-checked across sources.

title: Gurjan / Keruing Wood (Dipterocarpus): Properties & Uses
summary: Gurjan / keruing (Dipterocarpus spp.): a heavy tropical hardwood ~745 kg/m3, used as structural plywood face and core for container flooring and heavy-duty ply.

title: Rubberwood (Hevea brasiliensis): Properties, Density & Uses
summary: Rubberwood (Hevea brasiliensis): a medium-weight plantation hardwood at ~595 kg/m3, used for interior furniture, plywood cores and packing board. Must be treated.

title: Eucalyptus Wood: Properties, Density & Uses
summary: Eucalyptus (Eucalyptus spp.): a dense plantation hardwood at ~640 kg/m3 for E. grandis, widely peeled for plywood cores, LVL and packing-grade panels.

title: Poplar Wood (Populus): Properties, Density & Uses
summary: Poplar (Populus spp.): a light pale hardwood, ~415-450 kg/m3, that peels cleanly for packing, matchwood and core-veneer plywood. Properties, density and uses.

title: Birch Wood (Betula): Properties, Density & Uses
summary: Birch (Betula spp.): pale northern hardwood at ~600-690 kg/m3, non-durable, the basis of Baltic birch plywood and high-grade decorative veneer.

title: Jackwood (Artocarpus heterophyllus): Properties & Uses
summary: Jackwood (Artocarpus heterophyllus): a medium-density Kerala hardwood, ~420-710 kg/m3, naturally termite-resistant, used for furniture, joinery and packing-case framing.

title: Mango Wood (Mangifera indica): Properties, Density & Uses
summary: Mango wood (Mangifera indica) is a medium-density Indian hardwood, ~675 kg/m3, used for interior furniture, plywood, veneer and light packaging such as pallets and crates.

title: Silver Oak (Grevillea robusta): Properties & Uses
summary: Silver oak (Grevillea robusta): a South-Indian plantation hardwood at ~570 kg/m3, used for packing timber, tea-chest work and utility plywood cores.

title: Pine Wood (Pinus): Properties, Density & Uses
summary: Pine (Pinus spp.): a light-to-medium softwood, ~350-660 kg/m3, the world's workhorse for pallets, crates and packing plywood, easy to heat-treat for export.

title: Teak Wood (Tectona grandis): Properties, Density & Uses
summary: Teak (Tectona grandis): golden-brown hardwood, ~650 kg/m3, very durable heartwood for boat decking, marine joinery and outdoor furniture. Data sheet and uses.

title: Sheesham (Dalbergia sissoo): Properties, Density & Uses
summary: Sheesham (Dalbergia sissoo): dense North-Indian rosewood ~770 kg/m3, Janka 1,660 lbf. Durable, decorative furniture, joinery and veneer hardwood.

title: Sal Wood (Shorea robusta): Properties, Density & Uses
summary: Sal (Shorea robusta) is a very heavy Indian hardwood, ~880 kg/m3, naturally durable and termite-resistant. Used for railway sleepers, beams and heavy structural work.

title: Neem Wood (Azadirachta indica): Properties & Uses
summary: Neem (Azadirachta indica): a dense ~800 kg/m3 Indian hardwood with naturally termite- and decay-resistant heartwood, used for furniture and agricultural timber.

title: Mahogany (Swietenia / Toona): Properties, Density & Uses
summary: Mahogany data sheet: true Swietenia (590-640 kg/m3) and Indian toona (450-510 kg/m3), the classic reddish-brown cabinet hardwood for veneer, furniture and plywood faces.
